claim_bullets.bullet_text: Generators in JavaScript allow for simpler code when dealing with recursive iterations or state patterns, as they implicitly manage state (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.224-245).
JavaScript generators are functions defined with function* syntax that use yield or yield* to generate values, making them useful for creating iterables (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.224-245).
Generators can be used to implement state machines like the Fibonacci sequence more naturally than traditional iterators (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.224-245).

source_record_text: Chapter on generating iterables from JavaScript Allongé, covering iterators, generators, and their use in handling recursive data structures and state machines.
